title: Choosing products with recycled content
subtitle: What recycled content labels mean and which categories have the most material impact
excerpt: Recycled content in a product means that a proportion of the raw material came from post-consumer or post-industrial waste rather than virgin extraction. The carbon saving varies substantially by material: recycled aluminium uses 95% less energy than virgin; recycled plastic uses 70-80% less; recycled steel uses 55-65% less.
sourceNotes: WRAP guidance on recycled content standards and labelling. Aluminium Stewardship Initiative data on recycled aluminium energy savings. Ellen MacArthur Foundation circular economy metrics on recycled content impact.

content.text: The most impactful categories for buying
content matters most in three cases. The carbon saving against virgin material is large. The product is big, or you replace it often. A recycled version is on the shelf at about the same price.

Recycled aluminium uses about 95% less energy than virgin aluminium. Look for cookware, food tins, and packaging that state a recycled content. Aluminium is closed-loop. It can be recycled again and again without loss of quality. Buying aluminium and putting it in the recycling at end-of-life beats avoiding aluminium altogether.
Recycled plastic (rPET, rHDPE) cuts virgin plastic demand. You find it in clothing (fleece from bottles), garden products, and packaging. Most recycled plastic is

rather than closed-loop. The chain reaches landfill or incineration in the end. The extra life still cuts virgin extraction. Look for the percentage recycled content on garden furniture, decking, and outdoor products. 50 to 100% recycled content is on offer in most of these lines.

Recycled paper and cardboard uses 25 to 50% less energy than virgin-fibre paper. It also skips the need for fresh forest fibre. FSC and PEFC labels on virgin paper signal well-managed forestry. Recycled content skips the forest impact altogether. Office paper, notebooks, and packaging are the easiest places to find recycled content at the same price as virgin.
